items.title: 「グッチ フローラ」が新フレグランス発売 香水着想の限定メイクアップコレクションも
items.summary: Gucci Beauty announced Flora Gorgeous Orchid Eau de Parfum Intense, the highest-concentration entry in the Flora line, launching in Japan September 1 with a vanilla-orchid profile, priced JPY 5,170 (10mL) to JPY 24,970 (100mL). A limited 10-shade eyeshadow palette and two lipsticks accompany the launch; Miley Cyrus fronts the campaign, continuing her multi-year role as Flora's face.

items.title: 古驰美妆提前"入列"欧莱雅：27亿交接背后，高端美妆香水赛道再升温
items.summary: L'Oreal will take over the Gucci Beauty license a year early, from July 2027, under a new 50-year exclusive deal; L'Oreal absorbs roughly 70% of early-termination and inventory-transition costs, while outgoing licensee Coty receives about $400M compensation. The piece situates the move within L'Oreal China's 5.6% Q1 luxury-division growth and flags analyst concern about internal brand overlap with Lancome and Helena Rubinstein.

items.title: 双G标志为什么能成为经典？百年古驰做对了这件事
items.summary: Brand-history feature tracing Gucci's 1921 Florence founding and the double-G/Horsebit motifs, crediting Alessandro Michele's 2015-era repositioning for shifting the house toward younger, creativity-driven consumers on three pillars of creativity, Italian craftsmanship and innovation. Largely promotional in tone with no critical framing, illustrating the brand-marketing skew common in Chinese trade coverage.

items.title: 微博网友围绕古驰百年双G标志文章展开讨论
items.summary: Weibo/social chatter tied to the July 12 double-G brand-history piece shows engagement clustered around nostalgia for Alessandro Michele-era maximalism versus curiosity/skepticism toward Demna's more restrained direction; several threads note Gucci's official Weibo content remains centered on celebrity endorsement and product posts rather than narrative storytelling, echoed by commentators as lacking novelty.

items.source: Daum (via community aggregation, 명품 리셀 시장 기사)
items.title: 중고시장 뜨는데 중고 명품은 외면…신뢰 잃은 '럭셔리 리셀'의 추락
items.summary: Korean consumer-facing piece drawing on resale-market/community sentiment reports Kering's top three brands saw secondhand value drops over the past year -- Gucci -10%, Balenciaga -14%, Bottega Veneta -23% -- attributing the resale slump to post-pandemic savings depletion, high interest rates reducing investment-holding incentive, and growing consumer skepticism ("buying at retail is more rational") once resale markups became widely understood via social media.

items.title: 명품 갤러리 - 구찌 관련 스레드 모음
items.summary: Korean luxury-forum (DCInside myungpum gallery) threads mixing replica/counterfeit comparison discussion with general Gucci commentary; recurring theme is price-versus-value skepticism among everyday posters rather than press-driven brand narrative, consistent with broader forum sentiment toward repeated luxury price hikes.
